objective: Before-and-after case-study Reel proving Pitch Nav works over time, structurally distinct (split-screen throughout) from the other four launch Reels.
audience: Parents/athletes evaluating whether the product produces real, trackable change — mid-to-lower funnel.
coreInsight: Front-side stability at foot strike visibly improved over six weeks of drill work targeting that exact priority, and timing is the next thing to work on.
openLoop: The split-screen opening promises a real comparison — the viewer watches to see what actually changed.
payoff: The synchronized comparison and overlay show the specific improvement, and an honest 'still needs work' beat makes the case study credible.

scenes.cameraDirection: Split screen, two real clips of the same athlete, both paused at the same starting pose.
scenes.soundDesign: Single low tick on the cut in.

assetReferences.description: Athlete C, clip from week 0, side view
Athlete C, clip from week 6, side view, matched framing

scenes.cameraDirection: Both clips begin playing in sync from leg lift, still split screen.
scenes.voiceOver: These two clips are six weeks apart.

scenes.cameraDirection: Freeze both clips at front-foot strike; blue overlay highlights front-leg/hip stability on both sides.
scenes.voiceOver: Watch the front leg at foot strike.
scenes.soundDesign: Freeze-frame stop accent, both sides simultaneously.

assetReferences.description: Blue overlay on front-leg/hip stability, both sides

scenes.cameraDirection: Both clips resume in sync, slow motion, playing through release, matched frame-for-frame, still split screen.
scenes.voiceOver: Same pitch. Watch how the front side holds up differently.

scenes.cameraDirection: Freeze both on release; overlay comparison shows the improved stability angle on the 'after' side.
scenes.voiceOver: The front side is noticeably more stable at release now.

assetReferences.description: Comparison overlay showing improved stability angle

scenes.cameraDirection: Cut to a single, non-split clip of the most recent rep — honest, not celebratory.
scenes.voiceOver: Timing still needs work — that's the next priority.
scenes.soundDesign: Clean cut, music dips slightly for the honest beat.

assetReferences.description: Most recent rep, single frame, not split screen

scenes.cameraDirection: Screen recording of the progress-tracking calendar spanning the six-week window, priority updated.
scenes.voiceOver: Pitch Nav tracked the whole six weeks.

assetReferences.description: Progress calendar spanning the six-week window

scenes.cameraDirection: Static end card: wordmark small at top, CTA centered.

voiceOverScript: These two clips are six weeks apart. Watch the front leg at foot strike. Same pitch. Watch how the front side holds up differently. The front side is noticeably more stable at release now. Timing still needs work — that's the next priority. Pitch Nav tracked the whole six weeks. Start your Pitch Nav analysis.
bRollInstructions: Both clips must be real footage of the same, real, consenting athlete, genuinely six weeks apart, matched framing/angle so the split screen lines up honestly. Do not simulate the 'after' clip.
editingNotes: Split-screen persists through most of the runtime — the only Reel in this set structured that way. Frame changes every 1-3 seconds. The 'what still needs work' beat (0:18-0:21) is mandatory — do not cut it even under time pressure, per content/video-templates/formats/format-b-before-and-after.md.
soundDesign: Uplifting, gradual build — a progress/achievement feel, but restrained. Music dips slightly during the honest 'timing is next' beat, then resumes building into the product-output and CTA beats.

assetReferences.description: Athlete C, two clips six weeks apart, matched side-view framing
Progress calendar spanning the six-week window
Blue stability overlay, both freeze-frame comparisons
